{"query":"Rivian Autonomy+ self driving service one time price monthly subscription cost release date 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1435,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://rivian.com/autonomy","title":"Rivian Autonomy+: Universal Hands-Free, Co-steer, and AI innovation","content":"Enjoy hands-free assisted driving on 3.5 million miles of roads in the US and Canada.
Award applies to 2026 Rivian R1S
All new R1S and R1T deliveries include a 60-day trial.
Review the Universal Hands-Free software release notes and Rivian Owner's Guide for additional detail about this feature when available.
One-time purchase upgrade product features remain available during the lifetime of feature support for the hardware on the vehicle at delivery."},{"url":"https://electrek.co/2026/06/17/rivian-ceo-self-driving-prices-fall-tesla-fsd/","title":"Rivian CEO signals self-driving prices, like Tesla's, will fall | Electrek","content":"A $2,500 feature that BYD gives away free
Tesla, by comparison, charged $8,000 for FSD before killing the one-time purchase option in February and going subscription-only at $99 per month.
In China, BYD bundles its “God’s Eye” driver-assistance suite into its cars at no extra cost.
“I think this will become a feature that’s embedded in the purchase price.
Tesla has spent years telling customers to buy FSD now because the price would “only go up” as the software improved, and CEO Elon Musk has signaled the $99 subscription will rise as the system moves toward unsupervised driving.
We’ve covered how Tesla’s shift to subscription-only FSD and its decision to paywall basic Autopilot point the opposite direction — toward extracting more recurring revenue, not less.
Scaringe’s broader point is about cost competition with China, not just feature pricing.
The full self-driving hardware — Rivian’s in-house 1,600 TOPS silicon, better cameras, and lidar — arrives in R2 models from late 2026.
Stay up to date with the latest content by subscribing to Electrek on Google News."},{"url":"https://eletric-vehicles.com/rivian/rivian-starts-charging-for-hands-free-driving-after-extending-free-trial-three-times/","title":"Rivian Starts Charging for Hands-Free Driving After Extending Free Trial Three Times","content":"Apr 6, 2026 — The software is available as a $49.99 monthly subscription or a one-time purchase of $2,500 in the United States, and C$69.99 per month or C$3, ...
The software is available as a $49.99 monthly subscription or a one-time purchase of $2,500 in the United States, and C$69.99 per month or C$3,500 in Canada.
The autonomy features are only available for second-generation R1 vehicles, with Gen 1 units having the more limited Driver+ assisted driving.
Rivian‘s autonomy software currently offers ‘Universal Hands-Free,’ ‘Highway Assist,’ ‘Adaptive Cruise Control’ and ‘Lane Change on Command’ features.
Earlier this year, the company again delayed the timeline for the paid service “through April 4.”
Rivian’s driver-assistance software currently falls between Tesla’s Autopilot (L1) and the more advanced Full-Self Driving (Supervised), which is gradually moving toward Level 3 autonomy.
Under the agreement, Uber will invest up to $1.25 billion in Rivian through 2031, and the two companies plan to deploy up to 50,000 fully autonomous robotaxis across 25 cities in the United States, Canada, and Europe.
Rivian has guided for between 62,000 and 67,000 total vehicle deliveries in 2026, of which the R2 is expected to account for about 20,000 to 25,000 units."}]}
